channel.title: Home - ChessBase India
channel.description: Read every news about chess in detail with ChessBase India in English. Read English news related with Chess such as Tournament, Players, every report of the Match.

item.title: When Kohli faced Sharma on the chessboard
item.description: For many years, Indian sports fans have debated Kohli vs Sharma. Cricket fans get excited whenever these two iconic surnames appear on opposite sides. But a Kohli-Sharma clash on a chessboard? That is not something we have seen before. So how exactly did this showdown find its way onto 64 squares? Check out the article to find out.

item.title: Shamsiddin Vokhidov Defeats Nepomniachtchi to Gain the Sole Lead at UzChess Cup
item.description: Shamsiddin Vokhidov is in a red-hot form; he scored his 3rd consecutive win of the Uzchess Cup Masters 2026 in the 5th round. Vokhidov defeated Ian Nepomniachtchi with the white pieces to gain the sole lead with 3.5/5 points. Mukhiddin Madaminov is also playing very exciting chess, as all 5 of his games were decisive without a single draw. In the 5th round, he defeated Shakhriyar Mamedyarov with black pieces. The clash between the top-2 seeded Arjun and Abdusattorov ends in a draw. Photo: UzChess Cup

item.title: Audi Ameya & Iniyan in Joint Lead After 2 Rounds at 360 One Global Chess Masters 2026
item.description: After completion of 2 rounds at the 360 One Global Chess Masters 2026, just 4 players are on a perfect 2/2. Iniyan Panneerselvam defeated IM Nikhil Dixit on the top board. Audi Ameya defeated IM De Silva L M S T, who had a big upset yesterday by defeating Sankalp Gupta. Alexei Fedorov and Alexander Slizhevsky are the other two players on full score. In the Challengers and Junior Masters, top-seeded Madhvendra Pratap Sharma is co-leading with 2/2 points. Check out the Puzzle of the Day from the tournament in the report. Photos: Ayush Jain/ ChessBase India

item.title: GMs Wanted! Pune’s Historic 1st Grandmaster Round Robin is Searching for 1 Indian and 1 Foreign GM
item.description: The city of Pune is gearing up for a historic chess event. Organised by the Buddhibal Kreeda Trust (BKT), Pune's first-ever Grandmaster Norm Round Robin Tournament is officially scheduled to take place from June 30th to July 8th, 2026. To complete this elite field and optimise the norm opportunities for the contenders, the organisers are currently seeking two Grandmasters to join the field. Organisers are offering very good conditions for GM title players along with suitable appearance Money. Apply Fast!!

item.title: Massive Round 1 Upsets at 360 One Global Chess Masters 2026
item.description: The 9th Edition of 360 One Global Chess Festival kicks off in Mumbai. The tournament features 3 sections: Masters, Challengers, and Junior Masters. In the Masters section, a total of 56 players are taking part, and the rating average of the tournament is 2356, which makes this tournament one of the strongest open of India and gives a lot of chances to players to score norms. Check out what happened on the 1st day. Photos: Ayush Jain/ ChessBase India

item.title: Arjun draws against Vidit, shares lead with Vokhidov and Abdusattorov
item.description: After 4 rounds of play at the UzChess Cup Masters 2026, top-seeded Nodirbek Abdusattorov is co-leading with Arjun Erigaisi and Shamsiddin Vokhidov. In the 3rd round, there was only one decisive result where Shamsiddin defeated Madamiov. He continued his good run to score his second consecutive win in the 4th round against Nikolas Theodorou. In another important result of the 4th round, Yakubboev gave Madaminov his 2nd loss of the event. Photo: UzChess Cup

item.title: Join Hindi ChessBase India – We are Hiring
item.description: Are you passionate about chess? Do you enjoy writing, creating videos, managing social media, or bringing stories from the world of chess to a wider audience? If your answer is "yes," then Hindi ChessBase India has an exciting opportunity for you. Chess is growing rapidly in India, and the Hindi-speaking chess community is expanding every day. Our mission is to bring chess events, tournaments, players, and inspiring stories from around the world to millions of readers and viewers in Hindi.

item.title: Ankit Kumar Singh Wins 1st G H Raisoni Memorial Katni Rapid Rating 2026
item.description: Ankit Kumar Singh of Jharkhand scored an unbeaten 8.5/9 points to win the 1st G H Raisoni Memorial Katni Rapid Rating 2026. The next 5 places were clinched by local players of Madhya Pradesh. Muralilal Kori scored 8/9 points to finish in 2nd place. 4 players, including Vaibhav Tomar, Viabhav Nema, Harshit Saraf, and Harish Phulwani, scored 7.5/9 to secure 3rd to 6th places according to tiebreak scores. The tournament was organised at Arindum Hotel, Katni, on 31st May 2026. The Total prize fund was ₹2,00,000, with the top 3 prizes of ₹31,000, ₹25,000, and ₹20,000, along with trophies. Photos: Vikas Sahu and Mosuf Ahmad Bittu

item.title: How Chessworld AI is building the future of chess analytics
item.description: What happens to thousands of chess games after a tournament ends? For most players, they remain locked away on scoresheets and are never revisited. Chessworld AI, founded by Damodar Mundrikeri, is working to change that by digitizing games and turning them into valuable learning tools. The startup is building a data-driven ecosystem for players, coaches, parents, and organizers. Read their story of how they are trying to shape the future of chess analytics in India. Photos: Chessworld AI

item.title: Guess Who Won? TN CM Vijay Plays Chess With Praggnanandhaa
item.description: In any player’s journey, talent and hard work are only half the battle. The other half is the belief and support they receive from the world around them. When R Praggnanandhaa returned to India after becoming the first-ever Indian to win the prestigious Norway Chess 2026, defeating World No.1 Magnus Carlsen twice in the same event. He was welcomed with a massive gesture of encouragement. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay felicitated the young prodigy, presenting him with a cash award of ₹50 lakh on behalf of the Sports Development Authority of Tamil Nadu (SDAT).

item.title: Shankar Ramakrishnan Wins 6th CFPD National Chess Championship 2026 for Specially Abled Players
item.description: They say chess is a game played entirely in the mind, but at the 6th CFPD National Chess Championship 2026, it was really a celebration of the human spirit. This special tournament brought together the country’s most determined specially abled players. Physical challenges completely disappeared the moment the games began. In a thrilling finish, Tamil Nadu’s Shankar Ramakrishnan played brilliantly, scoring 7.5 out of 9 points to become the champion. Read more about Bravehearts and their achievements. Photos: IM Akshat Khamparia

item.title: A formal complaint against Pragg and Vaishali!
item.description: Praggnanandhaa wins Norway Chess. Vaishali wins the WR Women's Chess Tour in Tokyo. Parents everywhere are impressed. Siblings everywhere are worried. The golden siblings of Indian chess have achieved so much that they have accidentally raised the bar for brothers and sisters across the world. Shortly after their achievements, we received an unexpected letter. We publish this letter in this article for our readers.

item.title: UzChess Cup Masters 2026: Arjun Erigaisi Beats Yakubboev; Vidit Gujrathi Falls to Nepo
item.description: The 3rd UzChess Cup Masters 2026 has kicked off in Tashkent, Uzbekistan. The top seed at the Masters is the local hero Nodirbek Abdusattorov, while India's No.1 Arjun Erigaisi is the 2nd seed. The first 2 round of the tournament are done, and Mukhiddin Madaminov, who is also the second of the Candidates winner Javokhir Sindarov, is leading the tournament with 2/2 points after scoring a huge win over Ian Nepomniachtchi in the 1st round. Arjun Erigasi is on 1.5/2 after defeating Nodirbek Yakubboev in 2nd round. Photo: Uzbekistan Chess Federation

item.title: Samuel Sevian Wins 7th Stepan Avagyan Memorial 2026, Nihal Sarin Finishes Third
item.description: Samuel Sevian scored an unbeaten 5.5/9 points to win the 7th Stepan Avagyan Memorial Round Robin Chess Tournament 2026. This was Sam's second time winning this event. In the final round, he defeated the defending champion, Aravindh Chithambaram, to clinch the title. Sam Sevian and Vladislav Artemiev scored 5.5/9 to finish in 1st and 2nd place according to the tiebreak score. Nihal Sarin scored 5/9 points to finish in 3rd place. Photo: Official website

item.title: India's Savitha Shri Baskar Becomes The Asian Women's Champion 2026
item.description: IM Savitha Shri Baskar scored an unbeaten 7.5/9 points to become the Asian Women's Individual Chess Champion 2026. Savitha started the tournament on the 6th rank with a rating of 2374. She played tremendous chess and scored 2 consecutive wins in the last 2 rounds to clinch the title. Savitha played with a performance rating of 2555 and increased rating by 19.5 points, taking it to 2395! With this win, Savitha also qualified for the FIDE Women's World Cup 2027.
